# PIQI Group — World-Class Web Design Research Dossier

> A permanent reference artifact for the PIQI Group redesign. Synthesized from eight research lenses (award-winning craft, product/brand taste, immersive/interactive technique, motion systems, typography & color, house-of-brands IA, performance/a11y/modern CSS, and premium dark aesthetics). Every recommendation is reconciled against PIQI's locked constraints in the final section. Goal: elevate a competent-but-conventional dark site to Awwwards-SOTD-adjacent quality **without** breaking a single locked decision and **without** real copy (placeholder/lorem only).

---

## 1. Executive Summary — What "World-Class" Means for PIQI

- **One signature moment, polished to glass — not ten gimmicks.** Every Site-of-the-Year winner (Igloo Inc, Control, Cuberto) wins on a *single* unforgettable, brand-true beat executed flawlessly, with ruthless restraint everywhere else. For PIQI, **the red is the asset**: the living red→navy→ink hero gradient or a branded route-transition wipe should *be* the signature — not a 3D scene we have no assets for.
- **Awards reward taste and choreography, not polygon count.** Cuberto and Obys win Awwwards with almost no WebGL. The cheap-but-premium 80% (Lenis smooth scroll, scroll-driven CSS reveals, magnetic cursor, shader-on-a-plane hover, grain + glow overlays, view-transition crossfades) gets us to SOTD-adjacent at a fraction of the bundle and with **zero static-generation regression**.
- **"Expensive" is a system, not an effect.** A strict 4→192px spacing scale, negative-tracked display type over small confident body, low-alpha-white hairlines instead of solid boxes, one editorial accent, spring/expo-eased transform-only motion, and organized density (stat rows, numbered indices). What you *omit* (no second accent, no gradient on interactive surfaces) is the luxury signal.
- **Premium dark is layered light, not an inverted light theme.** A tonal elevation ladder (never pure `#000`), hairline edges that catch light, a single soft red bloom per viewport, mandatory `feTurbulence` grain to kill banding, and generous 120–200px section pacing. These are the single biggest "tells" separating amateur from agency-tier dark sites.
- **The IA must read house-of-brands at the portfolio level, branded-house at the chrome.** Six self-contained vertical worlds inside one persistent PIQI shell. The octopus is hub-and-spoke with a hard two-depth ceiling; asymmetry (only Consulting is a mini-hub) is correct and should be *visible* — never fake symmetry the sitemap doesn't have.
- **Performance and accessibility are scored, not optional.** Awwwards weights ~30% usability. Core Web Vitals (LCP < 2.5s, INP < 200ms, CLS < 0.1) are design constraints. The hero must be static/CSS for first paint with any JS hydrating *after* LCP; `prefers-reduced-motion` and contrast-verified red gate everything.
- **Modern CSS is the unlock for this stack.** Container queries, `:has()`, subgrid, scroll-driven animations (`animation-timeline`), View Transitions, `color-mix()`, `clamp()`, and `@property` deliver award-tier behavior with **zero new dependencies**, all token-driven in `globals.css`, all dark-only/no-Tailwind compatible — every one wrapped in `@supports` + reduced-motion guards.

---

## 2. Theme Sections

### 2.1 Award-Winning Craft

**The meta-truth:** Top juries (Awwwards SOTD, FWA, CSSDA) reward a single unforgettable signature moment wrapped in restraint. Igloo Inc (Site of the Year 2024, full WebGL world by Abeto/Vicente Lucendo: Three.js + three-mesh-bvh + Svelte + GSAP + Vite, procedurally grown ice crystals, shader-driven UI text) won because the *whole interface is one cohesive world*, not a stack of effects. Control rendered headline text to a canvas, turned it into a texture, and ran it through a custom distortion shader to rebuild on load/scroll — the dominant 2024–26 hero pattern.

**Techniques and named references:**
- **Full-bleed WebGL/canvas hero with shader-distorted text;** kinetic type-as-hero (variable-font headlines stretching/weight-shifting on scroll/cursor proximity — Glossier, Samsung); layered parallax depth off one scroll loop.
- **Scroll choreography:** **Lenis** (darkroom.engineering) is the de facto smooth-scroll lib; everyone syncs **GSAP ScrollTrigger** + WebGL off Lenis's single RAF loop. Pinned scroll-scenes (scrollytelling), horizontal segments inside vertical pages (State of AI 2025).
- **Custom cursors:** magnetic/elastic (Cuberto's signature), dot+trailing-ring, blend-mode invert, text-label ("View"/"Drag"), flashlight reveal.
- **Page transitions:** Barba.js/Swup/Taxi.js for non-framework; in React/Next the **View Transitions API** + framer-motion/GSAP. Winners kill white-flash — content cross-fades, a panel wipes, or a shared element morphs (FLIP + reveal masks).
- **Loaders:** counter (0→100, kinetic), logo mask-reveal, brand-color curtain lift — doubles as the first signature moment and buys WebGL/font init time.
- **Grid-breaking:** broken/asymmetric grids over a *hidden* grid system; oversized editorial numerals; type bleeding off-canvas.
- **Kinetic type:** variable fonts animating `wght`/`wdth` on scroll/hover; marquee bands; per-character stagger (GSAP SplitText / CSS `@property` + clip).
- **Sound:** subtle UI ticks/whooshes with a persistent mute (Dogstudio hallmark) — niche, low priority for a corporate umbrella.

**Studios worth studying:** Igloo Inc, Control, Cuberto, Locomotive, Active Theory, Resn, Dogstudio (DEPT), Obys Agency, Noomo, Gentlerain AI, Vide Infra, OFF+BRAND, darkroom.engineering, Joffrey Spitzer (Astro + GSAP — proof you don't need 3D to win).

**The 10 most-worth-stealing (constraint-safe, PIQI-ranked):** (1) Lenis + ScrollTrigger off one RAF loop; (2) one signature hero shader OR pure-CSS animated gradient mesh behind the red gradient; (3) kinetic variable-font headline; (4) per-character/line mask-reveal via CSS clip-path keyframes; (5) magnetic cursor with `mix-blend-mode: difference`; (6) route transitions via View Transitions API + red wipe panel; (7) first-visit preloader (red curtain + counter + logo mask), sessionStorage-gated; (8) pinned scrollytelling for the octopus-verticals reveal; (9) asymmetric/broken vertical-card grid over a hidden CSS grid; (10) micro-delight tier with `prefers-reduced-motion` honored throughout.

### 2.2 Product / Brand Taste

The teardown of what makes marketing sites feel expensive and trustworthy:

- **Apple** — scroll as cinema: pinned sections, image-sequence reveals, optical centering, 96px+ → 17px type range, monochrome with one product-hero accent at a time. *Lesson: one statement gesture per section; never two accents fighting.*
- **Stripe** — animated WebGL mesh-gradient backdrop behind near-black, flawless optical alignment, 4/8px spacing, small confident body (Söhne-like). Trust = density done cleanly (API panels, logo walls, real numbers).
- **Linear** — the most-copied modern aesthetic: near-black canvas, Inter Variable ~510/590 weights with custom stylistic sets, one acid accent, 80–120px between sections, 8–12px intra-component gaps. **Avoids gradients on UI surfaces** — glow only as ambient blobs *behind* content. The exact model for editorial PIQI red.
- **Vercel (Geist)** — most directly transferable to a plain-CSS token system: Geist Sans + Mono, ink `#171717`, 12 spacing steps (4px→192px), 9 radii, 15 type styles, display `letter-spacing ~ -0.04em`, borders `rgba(255,255,255,0.08)`, 96px section padding, subtle blueprint grid, mono labels (`01 / SERVICES`). A near drop-in token spec.
- **Figma/Framer** — "canvas is alive," spring easing, magnetic buttons, marquees. *Motion should feel physical (spring), not linear-timed.*
- **Arc/Browser Company** — hand-drawn warmth proves "expensive" can be characterful, not only minimal.
- **Connoisseur tier:** Rauno Freiberg (2px micro-details: focus rings, `:focus-visible`, optically-corrected icons, reduced-motion); Family.co (buttery spring + transform-only scroll-linked physics); Cosmos.so (editorial masonry); Igloo (UI in WebGL to avoid style-recalc — aspirational only); the dev-tool dark cohort — Raycast (glowing accents, floating shadowed product frames, "the marketing page *is* the product"), Resend (extreme restraint, tiny gradient glints), Clerk (glassy layered shots), Superlist (bold spring + oversized type), Mintlify (doc-grade hierarchy = instant trust).

**What makes them feel expensive:** (1) a real 4→192px spacing scale used without exception (inconsistent gaps are the #1 amateur tell); (2) negative-tracked large display + small confident body; (3) borders/surfaces at low-alpha white, not solid grey (glass, not boxes); (4) one accent, editorial, glow *behind* content; (5) spring/transform-only motion with reduced-motion honored; (6) mono labels + numbering; (7) organized density; (8) micro-detail discipline.

### 2.3 Interactive / Immersive Moves — Tiered by Effort

**The 80/20 truth:** The expensive 20% (raw Three.js heroes) buys the last 20% of wow but costs a 150–600kb+ bundle, GPU/thermal load, a hard mobile-fallback story, and fights static generation (WebGL is client-only, must be `ssr: false` dynamic-imported). The cheap-but-premium 80% gets to SOTD-adjacent with a fraction of the bundle and no SSR/SSG regression.

**The stack the heavy end actually uses (for reference):** Lenis (smooth scroll, ~3kb, the one library to adopt); Three.js / R3F + drei + postprocessing; `14islands/r3f-scroll-rig` for DOM↔WebGL mapping; GLSL fragment shaders, now shifting to TSL (Three Shader Language, ships r167+, compiles to WGSL+GLSL); WebGPU ~95% coverage with WebGL2 fallback via `WebGPURenderer`; GPGPU compute shaders for 100k+ particle fields.

**Effect cost table:**

| Effect | Method | Cost | Degrades gracefully? |
|---|---|---|---|
| Cinematic 3D hero object (Lusion-style) | R3F + GLTF + PBR + postproc, scroll-scrubbed | Very high | Poorly — needs static poster |
| GPGPU particle field | Compute/FBO sim | High | Reduce count → OK |
| Fluid/metaball/distortion hover | Single fragment shader on a plane | Medium | Yes — fall back to CSS |
| Image RGB-split/lens distortion hover | Codrops shader plane | Medium | Yes |
| Scroll-synced reveals, typographic motion | GSAP + Lenis + clip-path | Low–medium | Yes |
| Magnetic cursor + trail | Canvas/SVG + JS lerp | Low | Yes (hide on touch) |
| Grain / gradient mesh / noise | CSS + tiny canvas or SVG `feTurbulence` | Very low | N/A |

**Tiered moves for PIQI:**

- **High-effort (pick ONE signature, or none):** a single R3F hero object on Home/About — an abstract architectural form with the red overlay baked into the material, scroll-scrubbed slow rotation, `ssr:false`, poster fallback, paused offscreen. OR a GPGPU particle field forming/dissolving the PIQI mark (only if you accept the bundle + fallback work).
- **Medium-effort (2–4, high ROI):** shader-on-a-plane hover (RGB-split/lens distortion revealing the red overlay) on each vertical hero; WebGL/canvas — or cheaper native View-Transitions — route crossfade; Obys-style scroll-pinned typographic sequences for the oversized red stat numbers; animated gradient-mesh/fluid red→navy overlay as a fragment shader behind hero text.
- **Low-effort (apply broadly — the 80% wow):** Lenis site-wide (one provider); magnetic custom cursor + hover trail (pointer-fine only); IntersectionObserver scroll-reveals (mask/clip-path, staggered, no library); SVG `feTurbulence` grain + animated red left-bar accents; `prefers-reduced-motion` branch on everything (accessibility *and* a free perf tier).

**Performance disciplines all heavy sites share:** one RAF loop (Lenis drives everything — never multiple schedulers); IntersectionObserver to pause WebGL offscreen (`frameloop="demand"`); disable smooth-scroll + WebGL on mobile/low-power (gate on `prefers-reduced-motion`, `matchMedia('(pointer: coarse)')`, `navigator.hardwareConcurrency`); cap `devicePixelRatio` at ~1.5–2; compress textures (KTX2/Basis), draco/meshopt GLTFs; server-render a poster so static HTML is never blank.

### 2.4 Motion System

**Easing — the curves pros ship (almost never the `ease` keyword):**
- `cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1)` — easeOutExpo, the single most-used: slide-ins, modals, section reveals (fast start, long luxurious settle).
- `cubic-bezier(0.22, 1, 0.36, 1)` — easeOutQuint, gentler sibling; hero/text reveals.
- `cubic-bezier(0.2, 0.8, 0.2, 1)` — punchy general UI (Vercel/Linear feel) for buttons/small state.
- `cubic-bezier(0.34, 1.56, 0.64, 1)` — back-out overshoot for energetic accents; use sparingly (overshoot reads cheap if overused on an editorial brand).
- `cubic-bezier(0.65, 0, 0.35, 1)` — easeInOutCubic for symmetric A↔B (carousels, tab indicators, the **red active-nav pill sliding**).
- **Rule:** enter = ease-out, exit = ease-in, move/reposition = ease-in-out; `linear` only for scroll-linked progress and continuous loops.

**Durations:** hover/border 120–200ms; button press/toggle 150–250ms; dropdown/tooltip 200–300ms; card lift/image zoom 300–450ms; section reveal 500–800ms; hero text/page-in 700–1100ms; full page/view transition 400–700ms. Premium tell = restraint: most UI at 180–300ms with `0.16,1,0.3,1`; anything over ~1s must be scroll-linked or a deliberate hero moment, never a blocking wait.

**Staggering:** children 40–80ms apart (60ms sweet spot); cap an 8–12 item cascade ≤ ~500ms; per-item Y offset 12–24px + opacity (not scale). Letter-by-letter 15–30ms/char, revealing whole words on long headlines to avoid typewriter drag.

**Scroll-linked vs scroll-triggered:** triggered = fires once on threshold cross (the workhorse fade-up, via IntersectionObserver, no library). Linked = progress bound to scroll position (parallax, pins, progress bars; must stay on compositor). Keep only ~10–20% of scroll motion scroll-*linked* — overuse causes the scroll-jacking fatigue judges penalize.

**Native CSS scroll-driven animations** (`animation-timeline: view()`/`scroll()`) — the big 2024–25 shift: scroll effects without JS, entirely on the compositor. `view()` = element-visibility (reveals); `scroll()` = container progress (bars, parallax). Shipped Chrome/Edge (late 2024) + Firefox (late 2025); **Safari does not yet support it** — wrap in `@supports (animation-timeline: view())` so non-supporting browsers show the static end-state.

**View Transitions API:** same-document (SPA) is Baseline newly-available as of late 2025 (Chrome/Edge 111+, Firefox 133+, Safari 18+) — safe with fallback. Cross-document (MPA `@view-transition { navigation: auto }`) is Chrome/Edge 126+ only; others snap harmlessly. 2025 additions: `view-transition-class`, `view-transition-name: match-element`, nested groups (Chrome 140), scoped `element.startViewTransition()`. **Next 14 App Router caveat:** the client router intercepts `<Link>` — use `next-view-transitions` (or experimental `viewTransition` flag) for SPA-style transitions. Override the default opacity crossfade with custom `::view-transition-old/new` keyframes for a branded red wipe.

**Spring physics:** stiffness/damping/mass instead of duration+easing. Premium presets: UI/snappy `{stiffness:400, damping:40}` (no visible bounce); soft/luxe `{stiffness:170, damping:26}`; drag-follow `{stiffness:300, damping:30}`. (Framer Motion's `{100,10,1}` default is too bouncy for editorial.) Springs shine for *interruptible* motion (drag, magnetic cursor, layout shifts); for deterministic brand reveals, stick with tuned cubic-beziers.

**Signature micro-interactions:**
- **Magnetic button:** on `pointermove`, translate toward cursor by `offset * 0.2–0.4`, lerp/spring back on leave; counter-move inner label ~0.15× opposite for the layered pull. ~15 lines, no library.
- **Text scramble:** cycle random glyphs per char resolving left-to-right over 600–1200ms; PIQI already has `scramble-text` — gate behind reduced-motion + IntersectionObserver to fire once on enter.
- **Hover:** clip-path/`background-position` wipes (`0.16,1,0.3,1`, ~400ms) read more premium than color fades; image hover `scale(1.04)` on `overflow:hidden` wrapper. Keep transforms GPU-only.

**Tooling for this stack:** Pure CSS + IntersectionObserver (0 KB, **primary**); Motion vanilla mini API (`animate`/`inView`/`scroll`, ~5–18 KB, for springs/scramble/magnetic — avoid the heavy React `motion` layer unless you need layout animations); GSAP + ScrollTrigger (~23+7 KB, now fully free, only if a pinned/horizontal hero demands it); Lenis (~3 KB, highest-ROI "award feel" upgrade — drive from one `gsap.ticker`, `lenis.on('scroll', ScrollTrigger.update)`, **disable under reduced-motion and on touch**). Target: Lenis + pure-CSS scroll-driven + Motion mini API keeps most of the site under ~10 KB JS.

**Drop-in `globals.css` motion tokens:**
```css
:root{
  --ease-out-expo: cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1);
  --ease-out-quint: cubic-bezier(0.22, 1, 0.36, 1);
  --ease-ui:        cubic-bezier(0.2, 0.8, 0.2, 1);
  --ease-in-out:    cubic-bezier(0.65, 0, 0.35, 1);
  --ease-back:      cubic-bezier(0.34, 1.56, 0.64, 1);
  --dur-1: 120ms; --dur-2: 200ms; --dur-3: 300ms; --dur-4: 450ms; --dur-5: 700ms;
  --stagger: 60ms; --reveal-y: 20px;
}
@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce){
  :root{ --dur-1:.01ms; --dur-2:.01ms; --dur-3:.01ms; --dur-4:.01ms; --dur-5:.01ms; --reveal-y:0px }
}
```
Defaults: buttons `--dur-2 var(--ease-ui)`; reveals `--dur-5 var(--ease-out-expo)` on a `view()` timeline (`animation-range: entry 0% cover 35%`); the red active-nav pill slides `--dur-3 var(--ease-in-out)` (a `view-transition-name` on the pill makes it glide between routes); stat count-ups `--dur-5`.

### 2.5 Typography & Color for Dark Themes

**Typography**

- **Fluid scale:** `clamp(MIN, rem-floor + vw-slope, MAX)` — the `rem` term guarantees zoom/a11y scaling, the `vw` term drives fluidity. Use **two ratios**: tight Minor/Major Third (1.2–1.25) for body/UI, dramatic Perfect Fourth→Golden (1.333–1.618) for display. Award sites push display `MAX` to 5–8rem `h1`. Generate slopes with a Utopia-style calculator, never eyeballed `vw`.
- **Variable fonts = infrastructure** (one file, every weight, animatable axes `wght`/`opsz`/`wdth`/`GRAD`). Fashionable now: display/editorial — *Editorial New*, *PP Neue Montreal*, *Monument Extended*, *Bricolage Grotesque* (free, variable), *GT Sectra*; body — *Söhne*, *Aeonik* (notably Virgin Hyperloop — apt for PIQI's Virgin-style umbrella), *GT America*, *Inter*, *Neue Haas Grotesk*; the 2026 "bouba grotesk" softly-humanist lean is the tech-brand default. **No-budget premium path:** Bricolage Grotesque (display) + Inter (body) — both free, variable, self-hostable via `next/font/local` (keeps routes static).
- **`font-optical-sizing: auto`** globally (thins hairlines, tightens spacing at display sizes — free with Inter/Bricolage).
- **Tracking:** display wants negative `-0.02em`→`-0.04em`; small all-caps eyebrows want positive `+0.08em`→`+0.12em`, often paired with the red.
- **Leading:** large display tight `0.95–1.05`; body `1.5–1.65`. Tie to tokens.
- **Editorial layout:** one expressive display face vs one neutral neo-grotesque body; asymmetric/broken grids, oversized numerals (red stats → 6–10rem, tabular figures, tight leading), poster-cropped headings, large section indices ("01 / 02") in mono or display face. Maps perfectly onto PIQI's existing red-stat and red-left-bar motifs.

**Color**

- **Layered near-black (never `#000`):** pure black amplifies saturation and causes halation around light text. Build a 4–5 step cool-undertoned near-black ramp (PIQI's navy lean suits this). Elevation = progressively lighter surfaces + hairline borders + subtle gradients, not drop shadows.
- **Single saturated accent:** `#bb181d` is a statement, not a utility. Body-level fills on dark should be desaturated ~20–30% to avoid vibration, but PIQI's *large, sparse* editorial uses (hero overlay, active pill, oversized stats, left-bars) keep full strength — exactly where it reads confident. Keep a brightened token for small text/links (base `#bb181d` is marginal for small text on near-black).
- **Gradients/glows/mesh/grain:** hero red→navy→ink linear over imagery; group pages get radial accent glows (ambient orb) instead of vertical imagery. Mesh OK but red must be the *only* hue (adding neon purple/pink = generic glassmorphism, dilutes PIQI). **Grain is mandatory** to kill banding on 8-bit displays: inline SVG `feTurbulence` (`baseFrequency ~0.6–0.9`, `numOctaves 3–4`), 3–8% opacity, `mix-blend-mode: overlay`/`soft-light`, `pointer-events:none`, fixed full-bleed — pure CSS, no JS, no asset.
- **Contrast:** body on near-black targets AA 4.5:1; use slightly-off white (`~#EDEDF0`) for body to reduce halation, reserve true white for headings. `#bb181d` passes for *large* text only — ship `--red-bright` for inline links/small UI.

**Recommended token set:**
```css
:root {
  --ink:#0a0b0e; --surface-1:#101218; --surface-2:#161922; --hairline:#242833;
  --text:#ededf0; --text-strong:#ffffff; --text-muted:#9aa0ad;
  --red:#bb181d; --red-bright:#e23b3f; --red-deep:#6e0f12;
  --overlay: linear-gradient(160deg, rgba(187,24,29,.72) 0%, rgba(16,18,30,.78) 52%, var(--ink) 100%);
  --step--1: clamp(0.83rem,0.80rem+0.15vw,0.94rem);
  --step-0:  clamp(1.00rem,0.95rem+0.25vw,1.13rem);
  --step-1:  clamp(1.25rem,1.15rem+0.5vw,1.50rem);
  --step-2:  clamp(1.56rem,1.35rem+1.0vw,2.25rem);
  --step-3:  clamp(1.95rem,1.55rem+2.0vw,3.38rem);
  --step-4:  clamp(2.44rem,1.70rem+3.7vw,5.06rem);   /* h1 */
  --step-stat: clamp(3.5rem,2rem+7vw,7.5rem);        /* red numerals */
  --lh-display:0.98; --lh-body:1.6; --tracking-display:-0.03em; --tracking-eyebrow:0.1em;
}
```

### 2.6 House-of-Brands IA

**The architectural choice:** PIQI is a thin-umbrella house of brands (Virgin model), but verticals are services *under* the PIQI name, not independently-famous brands (unlike LVMH Maisons). So the IA reads **house-of-brands at the portfolio level** (six distinct self-contained worlds) but **branded-house at the chrome level** (one persistent header, one footer sitemap, one design system). Avoid the *directory* failure mode (IAC/Berkshire: flat logo lists that link out, parent has no voice) — PIQI's verticals share a domain and should feel like one cohesive product.

**Homepage models for N verticals:** sector-grid/"our houses" index (LVMH/Accor — but with only 6, **no filtering UI needed**, the grid *is* the taxonomy); scroll-narrative pinned reveal (most award-tier, strongest elevation fit); manifesto-first (Virgin — sells the idea of the group first); index-as-homepage (Vox/Bonnier — too flat, skip). **Recommended:** manifesto hero (abstract motif) → scroll-narrative or interactive 6-tile grid → group facts/CTA.

**The octopus / hub-and-spoke rules:** PIQI (hub) → 6 vertical hubs (spokes); Consulting is the only vertical that is itself a mini-hub (5 sub-service spokes). **Two link depths max** (Group→Vertical=1, Consulting→sub-service=2); nothing deeper in v1; every route ≤2 clicks from home (also keeps all routes statically generatable). Sub-services are spokes of Consulting only — never promoted top-level. **Don't render expandable carets on childless verticals** — the nav must mirror the real octopus shape. Consulting needs an intra-hub local nav (sub-service rail/grid) the other five don't have; this asymmetry is correct and should be visible.

**Navigation:** mega-menu (LVMH/Accor) is an N=75 solution — borderline for 11 leaf destinations, over-promises uniform depth, adds engineering. **Recommended:** a minimal persistent header (Home · About · Contact · Verticals overview) with the red active-pill, giving **only Consulting** a flyout exposing its 5 sub-services, the homepage grid doubling as the overview surface.

**Per-vertical identity within cohesion:** LVMH's discipline — each property distinct yet in identical chrome. PIQI's lever is **one hero image per vertical + shared red overlay** (photo = identity, red = cohesion). Reinforce cheaply with a per-vertical scoped custom property, eyebrow label, and numbered index:
```css
.vertical[data-vertical="yachts"] { --hero: url(/brand/heroes/yachts.jpg); }
```
Keep identical section rhythm across all six hubs (Hero → UVP → offerings → FAQ → CTA) so they read as siblings.

**Wayfinding:** breadcrumbs earn their keep only at depth 2 — the 5 Consulting sub-service pages (`PIQI Group › Consulting › [Service]`, with `BreadcrumbList` JSON-LD). On the six hubs the active red pill already locates the user; add breadcrumbs there only for SEO parity. **Footer = full sitemap** (three columns: 6 verticals · 5 Consulting sub-services nested under Consulting · group/legal) — makes the octopus legible without a mega-menu, gives every page a path to every sibling. **Sibling cross-link strip:** close each vertical hub with a "More from PIQI Group" module linking 2–3 sibling verticals — lateral (sibling↔sibling) and up (→group) only, never down into another vertical's internals (respects no-nesting in links too). This is the single highest-value mechanism making the thin umbrella *feel* connected.

### 2.7 Performance / A11y / Modern CSS

**Core Web Vitals (75th percentile, mobile + desktop):** LCP < 2.5s, INP < 200ms (replaced FID March 2024), CLS < 0.1. Only ~48% of mobile pages pass all three, so "good" differentiates. Awwwards scores ~30% usability. Winners stay fast despite heavy visuals by offloading to compositor/Web Workers/OffscreenCanvas, treating speed budgets as a design constraint, and keeping **the hero static/CSS for first paint** with WebGL/JS hydrating *after* LCP.

**Images:** AVIF first, WebP fallback (`formats:['image/avif','image/webp']`); `next/image` `priority` on exactly the 1–2 above-fold images (emits `fetchpriority="high"` + preload — documented 2.6s→1.9s LCP win); correct `sizes` (full-bleed = `100vw`; 3-up grid ≈ `(max-width:768px) 100vw, 33vw`) — the most-skipped lever; always reserve layout (width/height or `fill` + sized container) for CLS 0.

**Fonts:** self-host via `next/font` (subsets, inlines metric-matched fallback). `font-display: optional` for best CLS (or `swap` pragmatic default; never `block`). Metric overrides (`size-adjust`, `ascent/descent/line-gap-override`) kill font CLS — `next/font`'s `adjustFontFallback` does this automatically. Preload only 1–2 above-fold weights, subset Latin.

**Code-splitting:** `next/dynamic({ssr:false})` for client-only below-fold widgets (Lottie, scramble/revolver, future canvas); gate expensive mounts on IntersectionObserver; prefer CSS scroll-driven over JS scroll libraries where possible. PIQI's `lottie-panel`, `scramble-text`, `revolver-heading` are the obvious split candidates.

**A11y for a flashy dark site:** `prefers-reduced-motion: reduce` (WCAG 2.3.3) — *replace* large transforms/parallax with short opacity crossfades, don't strip motion entirely; `gsap.matchMedia()` to branch. Visible `:focus-visible` rings (red/white, obvious — never kill outlines on dark), logical tab order, skip-link to `<main>`, move focus to heading on route change. Dark contrast: AA 4.5:1 body, 3:1 large/UI; `#bb181d` on near-black passes large only (~4.3:1) — safe for oversized stats/headings, **not** body/small labels, and never small red on the navy mid-tone. Semantics: one `<h1>`/page, ordered headings, `<nav>`/`<main>`/`<footer>`, real `<button>`/`<a>` (not divs) for the active pill and CTAs, `alt` on vertical heroes.

**Keeping routes static:** a route flips to `ƒ Dynamic` when a **page or layout** calls a request-time primitive (`cookies()`/`headers()` at page/layout level, `searchParams`, `fetch(...,{cache:'no-store'})`, `export const dynamic='force-dynamic'`). Fix the root cause — push per-request logic (contact-form rate-limit, IP reads) into the Route Handler/Server Action. Rule: pages stay pure, dynamic work lives in `/api` handlers triggered from the client.

**Modern CSS toolbox (all Baseline-safe for a 2026 launch, zero new deps):**

| Feature | State | PIQI use |
|---|---|---|
| Container queries | Baseline 2023 | Vertical cards adapt to their slot, not viewport — one card works 1/2/3-up without breakpoints |
| `:has()` | Baseline | Style sections by children (hero `:has(video)` vs `:has(img)`), nav/form state — replaces JS class-toggling |
| Subgrid | ~97%, Baseline | Align card internals (eyebrow/title/body/CTA) across a row |
| Scroll-driven (`animation-timeline`) | Chrome 115+, Firefox late-2025; progressive | Reveal/parallax/progress off main thread, zero JS — highest award-feel ÷ cost; wrap in `@supports` + reduced-motion |
| View Transitions | cross-browser Oct 2025 | Cross-route/hero morphs; enhancement with clean fallback |
| `color-mix()` | Baseline | One-source tints: `color-mix(in oklch, var(--red) 80%, black)` |
| `clamp()` | Baseline | Fluid type/spacing, no breakpoint cascade |
| `@property` | Chromium/Safari solid, Firefox shipped | Animate gradients/angles (the red→navy overlay angle) with typed fallbacks |

Always pair the cutting-edge ones (scroll-driven, VT, `@property`) with `@supports` so non-supporting browsers degrade to static-but-correct.

### 2.8 Premium Dark-Aesthetic Details

Premium dark is layered light — disciplined control of how surfaces catch light, how edges are drawn, how restraint is enforced. The 12 "feels expensive" details:

1. **Tonal elevation ladder** — `--ink #0b0c0e` + `--surface-1 #121317` / `-2 #181a1f` / `-3 #1f2228`, each ~+4–6% lighter (Material's core dark rule: raised surfaces catch more ambient light, ~0%→16% white overlay). Base slightly desaturated cool-neutral (a whisper of navy). Pure `#000` reserved for full-bleed hero behind imagery only.
2. **Hairline system** — `1px solid rgba(255,255,255,.08)` borders + `inset 0 1px 0 rgba(255,255,255,.04)` top light-catch, never solid `#333` boxes. The "engineered precision" agency signature.
3. **SVG `feTurbulence` grain** — fixed, `opacity:.035`, `mix-blend-mode:overlay`, kills gradient banding (`baseFrequency 0.65–0.9`, `stitchTiles='stitch'`).
4. **Single red radial bloom** per hero/CTA at ≤15% opacity (glow, never fill): `radial-gradient(60% 50% at 50% 0%, rgba(187,24,29,.22), transparent 70%)`.
5. **Dark glassmorphism** — `backdrop-filter: blur(16px) saturate(1.2)` on a *tinted-dark* panel (not white frost) + hairline + faint inner top highlight; always `@supports not (backdrop-filter)` solid fallback. PIQI's fixed-header solid-glass state is exactly this.
6. **Two-layer soft shadows** — tight contact + long ambient low-opacity black, paired with the elevation overlay (shadow alone is invisible on dark, overlay alone is flat).
7. **Custom `::selection`** in PIQI red.
8. **Custom thin scrollbar** — near-invisible track, hairline thumb.
9. **Deliberate `:focus-visible` ring** — white/accent, offset; never browser blue, never `outline:none` without replacement.
10. **Generous vertical rhythm** — 120–200px section gaps, one idea per screen, asymmetric editorial layouts (cramped dark = SaaS dashboard, not luxury). Highest-leverage free change.
11. **Type polish** — `text-rendering: optimizeLegibility`, kerning/ligature `font-feature-settings`, oversized editorial display for vertical heroes.
12. **Accent budget** — red capped at 2–4 statement uses per page; everything else carried by tonal elevation, hairlines, grain. "When everyone goes bright, luxury goes dark."

**Vertical-specific premium cues:** Yachts — deep navy/obsidian, editorial white type, "casting off" immersive imagery motion (Wajer, Blue More, Burgess). Fashion — anti-design oversized editorial type, asymmetry, dramatic whitespace (exclusivity via type/layout confidence, not ornament). Auto — "Range Rover" register: sophisticated dark, magazine-spread product pages, matte-vs-gloss contrast, slow reveals. Group pages — abstract/architectural motifs only; carry premium via grain, glow, hairline grids, tonal elevation.

**Amateur tells to avoid:** pure `#000` (vibration on high-refresh, dead planes); one flat surface (no elevation ladder); low-opacity gray secondary text failing WCAG (#1 dark-mode a11y failure); solid heavy gray borders; accent cranked bright everywhere; inverted light-theme imagery punching bright holes; heavy/colored drop shadows that vanish or muddy; grain/blur cranked too high (dirty screen / smeary glass).

---

## 3. Principles Distilled

1. **One signature moment, glass-polished.** Pick a single brand-true beat (living red→navy hero, or a red route-wipe) and perfect it; cut every other gimmick.
2. **The red is the signature, not a 3D scene.** Lean on the asset you own; treat `#bb181d` as the differentiator.
3. **Restraint is the luxury signal.** What you omit (no second accent, no gradient on interactive surfaces, accent capped at 2–4 uses/page) reads as premium.
4. **Adopt a strict spacing scale (4→192px) and a 12–15 step fluid type scale.** Never freehand a gap; inconsistent gaps are the #1 amateur tell.
5. **Negative-track display, small confident body.** Display `-0.03em`, tight leading `~0.98`; body off-white `#ededf0` at `1.6`.
6. **Premium dark = layered light.** Tonal elevation ladder, hairline edges, never pure `#000` except behind hero imagery.
7. **Grain is mandatory.** SVG `feTurbulence` at ~3.5% kills banding and adds material.
8. **Glow behind content, never fill on surfaces.** A single soft red bloom ≤15% per viewport.
9. **Motion is spring/expo-eased and transform-only.** Enter ease-out, exit ease-in, move ease-in-out; most UI 180–300ms; stagger ~60ms.
10. **Prefer compositor-thread motion.** CSS scroll-driven + View Transitions over JS scroll libraries; one RAF loop (Lenis) if JS is needed.
11. **`prefers-reduced-motion` and contrast gate everything.** Replace (don't strip) motion; verify every red-on-dark pairing; red is large-text-only.
12. **Mono labels + numbering** (`01 — CONSULTING`) telegraph a systematized house of brands.
13. **Organized density = trust.** Stat rows, credential/logo wall, consistent radii, perfect focus rings.
14. **IA: house-of-brands portfolio, branded-house chrome, two-depth ceiling, visible asymmetry.** Octopus shape mirrored in nav; only Consulting is a mini-hub; footer is the full sitemap; sibling cross-links are lateral-only.
15. **Performance is a design constraint.** Static hero for LCP, JS hydrates after first paint, every advanced CSS wrapped in `@supports`, pages pure / dynamic work in `/api` — all 16 routes stay `○ Static`.

---

## 4. What Applies to PIQI (and What Does NOT)

Every recommendation reconciled against the locked constraints. Items needing a locked decision changed are flagged **REQUIRES USER APPROVAL**.

### Applies cleanly — no constraint conflict (plain CSS, dark-only, static-safe, zero/low new deps)

- **All §2.5 typography & color tokens, §2.4 motion tokens, §2.8 dark-aesthetic details** — pure `globals.css` custom properties, presentational, no route impact. Tonal elevation ladder, hairline system, `feTurbulence` grain, single red bloom, dark glassmorphism (with `@supports` fallback), two-layer shadows, custom `::selection`/scrollbar/`:focus-visible`, 120–200px rhythm, type polish, accent budget. **Adopt directly.**
- **Fluid `clamp()` type scale + two ratios + `font-optical-sizing: auto`** — plain CSS, on-brand for oversized red stats (6–10rem, tabular figures). **Adopt.**
- **Modern CSS toolbox** (container queries, `:has()`, subgrid, scroll-driven animations, View Transitions, `color-mix()`, `@property`) — all plain-CSS, token-friendly, dark-only compatible, no Tailwind. **Adopt, each wrapped in `@supports` + reduced-motion guards.** `color-mix()` is the right way to derive red tints from one source token.
- **Lenis smooth scroll + IntersectionObserver scroll-reveals + magnetic cursor (`mix-blend-mode: difference`) + scroll-pinned stat sequences** — client-island components (`'use client'`, dynamic-imported), plain CSS styling, routes stay static. Disable Lenis under reduced-motion and on touch. **Adopt (low-effort tier first).**
- **Branded route transition via View Transitions API + red wipe panel** — use `next-view-transitions` to wrap the App Router; override `::view-transition-old/new` with a red wipe. **Adopt, but verify all 16 routes remain `○ Static` after install** (the quality gate's explicit regression check).
- **First-visit preloader (red curtain + 0→100 counter + logo mask-reveal), sessionStorage-gated** — pure CSS/JS, on-brand. **Adopt as the signature first moment.**
- **House-of-brands IA** — minimal persistent header with red active-pill; Consulting-only flyout (no carets on childless verticals); homepage as the portfolio overview (manifesto abstract-motif hero → 6-tile grid → facts/CTA); identical 6-hub section template with per-vertical `data-vertical` scoped `--hero`; breadcrumbs only on 5 Consulting spokes (`BreadcrumbList` JSON-LD); footer as full sitemap; lateral-only "More from PIQI Group" sibling strip. **All static, all plain CSS, fully aligned with the octopus + no-nesting rules. Adopt.**
- **Per-vertical hero image + shared red overlay; group pages abstract/architectural/grain/glow/hairline-grid only** — this is *already* a locked PIQI rule; the research confirms it as best practice. **Reinforce.**
- **Performance/a11y disciplines** — AVIF/WebP, `priority` on 1–2 above-fold images, correct `sizes`, reserved layout, self-host fonts (`next/font/local`, `font-display: optional/swap`, metric overrides), `next/dynamic({ssr:false})` for `lottie-panel`/`scramble-text`/`revolver-heading`, pure server pages with dynamic work in `/api`. **Adopt** — directly serves the quality gate (all routes `○ Static`).
- **Existing `scramble-text` / `revolver-heading` as the signature gesture** — gate behind reduced-motion + IntersectionObserver (fire once on enter). **Lean into what already exists** rather than adding new effects.

### Applies with caution / partial

- **Kinetic variable-font headline (animate `font-variation-settings` on scroll/cursor)** — fine in plain CSS, but depends on self-hosting a variable font, scoped for **Phase 5**. Until then, the clip-path line-reveal works without it. **Adopt at Phase 5;** recommended free pairing **Bricolage Grotesque (display) + Inter (body)** via `next/font/local`. Note: changing the chosen typefaces from any already-specified brand font is a brand decision — confirm with the user before committing a family.
- **GSAP + ScrollTrigger** — only if a single pinned/horizontal hero genuinely demands it; otherwise pure-CSS scroll-driven + Lenis covers the need at lower bundle cost. **Add only on demonstrated need.**
- **Motion (vanilla mini API) for springs** — acceptable as a small client-island dep for magnetic/scramble/spring physics; avoid the heavy React `motion` component layer. Not CSS-in-JS (it animates the DOM imperatively), so no conflict with the no-CSS-in-JS rule. **Optional, justify per-use.**
- **Asymmetric/broken vertical-card grid** — achievable with CSS `grid-column` spans over a hidden grid; ensure it doesn't fight the "identical section rhythm across hubs" cohesion rule. **Adopt for the homepage tile grid; keep hub interiors consistent.**

### REQUIRES USER APPROVAL (would change a locked decision or add material cost/risk)

- **Any WebGL/Three.js/R3F hero or GPGPU particle field** (§2.1, §2.3 high-effort tier) — **REQUIRES USER APPROVAL.** It adds a 150–600kb+ client bundle, demands a poster fallback, and must be `dynamic(ssr:false)` to avoid flipping its route to `ƒ Dynamic`. The research's own verdict for a 16-page holding shell is *wrong cost/benefit*; the red gradient/shader-on-a-plane achieves the look far cheaper. If pursued, it must be a single island on Home/About only, poster-rendered server-side, paused offscreen, disabled on low-power — and the build must still show all 16 routes `○ Static`.
- **A canvas/WebGL fragment-shader hero behind the red gradient** (even lightweight OGL/2D-canvas) — **REQUIRES USER APPROVAL** as a new dependency + client island; verify no route regression. A pure-CSS animated gradient mesh (`@property`-animated angle + `feTurbulence` grain) is the no-approval alternative that should be tried first.
- **UI sound design / mute toggle** (§2.1) — low ROI for a corporate umbrella and introduces a new persistent global control; **REQUIRES USER APPROVAL** before building.
- **Adding any second hue** (neon purple/pink mesh, gold on yachts) — **violates the red-only statement rule; REQUIRES USER APPROVAL.** Default is red-as-only-hue.
- **Cross-document MPA View Transitions** (`@view-transition { navigation: auto }`) — conflicts with the App Router's `<Link>` interception and is Chrome-only; do not adopt the MPA variant. SPA-style via `next-view-transitions` is the approved path. (No approval needed to *avoid* it; flagged so no one wires up the MPA at-rule by mistake.)

### Explicitly does NOT apply (drop these)

- **Light theme / theme toggle / `[data-theme="light"]`** — removed on purpose; do not reintroduce under any "dark-mode best practice" framing.
- **Vertical imagery on group pages (Home/About/Contact)** — locked out; group pages = abstract/architectural/brand motifs only.
- **Nesting one vertical under another, or carets/flyouts on childless verticals** — violates the octopus + no-nesting rule; only Consulting is a mini-hub.
- **Full LVMH/Accor mega-menu** — an N=75 solution; over-promises uniform depth the octopus doesn't have. Use minimal header + Consulting-only flyout.
- **Tailwind, CSS-in-JS, per-component `.module.css` (unless genuinely scope-specific)** — locked out; every recommendation above is delivered in token-driven `globals.css`.
- **Real/fabricated copy** — all content stays placeholder/lorem; no team names, testimonials, vessel/pricing, or facts beyond `lib/constants.ts`. Any gap → TBD/TODO + Dependencies-table blocker.
- **Request-time primitives at page/layout level** (`cookies()`/`headers()`/`force-dynamic`) — would flip routes to `ƒ Dynamic` and fail the gate; keep them in Route Handlers/Server Actions.
